TextENGNew: ``All right, everyone who played 'Taraimawashi' -- thank you for your hard work!``
``This was the very first original story that wasn't in the source material. What did you think?@k@vS20/02/440200359. I'd be really happy if you enjoyed exploring this 'what if' scenario.``
``Enjoy it?!@k@vS20/04/440400275. With an ending that abrupt, I haven't the slightest clue what even happened!``
``...It was completely incomprehensible.``
``...Yeah, I'll say.@k@vS20/03/440300460. Before I knew it, everyone except me was dead~.``
``And even though I survived, I was so traumatized I couldn't string a sentence together...@k@vS20/03/440300462. I have absolutely no idea what happened myself.``
``Ahahaha, that's right.@k@vS20/02/440200361. This time, 'Taraimawashi' shows you one of the worst possible endings -- what happens when you avoid confronting the mystery altogether.``
``So it's a story full of things you can't understand, and the only thing you learn is the outcome of the tragedy.``
``...Mii.@k@vS20/05/440500168. For everyone to suddenly die in a disaster is a terrible story.``
``To begin with, how can the curse of Oyashiro-sama possibly cause a natural disaster?@k@vS20/04/440400277. It can't have any connection to the serial murders in the village.``
``Hmm~, that's a good point.@k@vS20/02/440200364. Ending with a pile of unsolved mysteries is really unsatisfying.``

``Well, mysteries are fine as mysteries.@k@vS20/03/440300464. ...But when there are this few hints or clues, deduction is basically impossible.@k@vS20/03/440300465. The question of 'who is the killer behind the serial murders?' got swapped out for 'what's the truth behind the village disaster?'``
``People who ended up on this route probably got angry and went, 'What even is this?'``
``Precisely.@k@vS20/04/440400279. And even before we got to the mystery, wasn't everyone acting strangely?``

``Mion-san got scary and snappish just because Shion-san and Keiichi-san were getting along.@k@vS20/04/440400281. And Rena-san was acting odd too -- she gave off this eerie feeling, like she was monitoring Keiichi-san.``
``...But the one acting most strangely was probably Keiichi.``
``Yeah, I felt the same way.@k@vS20/03/440300468. Kei-chan in this scenario was kind of pathetic -- desperately clinging to his peaceful everyday life... Honestly, it wasn't a good look.``

``Yep.@k@vS20/02/440200366. For instance, normally Keiichi-kun would never turn down Rena's invitation to go to the garbage dump.@k@vS20/02/440200367. After all, that's where the world of 'Higurashi' truly begins.``
``By avoiding that turning point where the mystery lay hidden, he essentially chose the most passive path possible.``
``...And it wasn't like him to just silently watch me leave when he could see I was feeling down, either.``
``The Keiichi-san who's usually so nosy and caring about all of us would naturally have found some excuse to walk home with me.``

``In that sense, maybe you could say this is the result of making out-of-character choices.@k@vS20/03/440300470. True to its name, 'Taraimawashi' -- the impression is of constantly running from the problem instead of facing it.``

``That said... an important character crucial to solving the mysteries of 'Higurashi' does appear in 'Taraimawashi.'@k@vS20/02/440200370. The detective named Akasaka-san who showed up at the end with Ooishi-san... do you remember, remember?``
``...He felt a bit out of place, like the polar opposite of Ooishi-san.@k@vS20/04/440400285. Though they seemed to know each other.``
``He was only there briefly at the end, but he gave off this elite, big-city detective vibe.@k@vS20/03/440300472. But... how does that connect to anything?``
``The person who actually has a connection with that detective is, believe it or not, Rika-chan.@k@vS20/02/440200372. Right, Rika-chan?``
``That's right. Nipah~``
``W-wait... Rika?@k@vS20/04/440400287. How did you end up getting to know a detective?``
``...The story of how we met is told in a public security case file called 'Himatsubushi.'@k@vS20/05/440500172. Detective Akasaka is the main character of that story.``
``By the way, what is this 'Himatsubushi' story about, exactly?``

``It's about the very beginning of the chain of events in Hinamizawa that came to be known as Oyashiro-sama's curse.@k@vS20/05/440500174. It takes place five years ago, during the dam opposition movement.@k@vS20/05/440500175. During an incident at that time, I meet my destined person.``
``D-destined person?!``
``R-Rika?!@k@vS20/04/440400289. Wh-what do you mean by that?!``
``You'll just have to play and find out. Nipah~``
``H-huuuuh~?@k@vS20/03/440300476. Way to leave us hanging!@k@vS20/03/440300477. Can't you just tell us without being so coy~?``
``Where's the fun in spoiling it?@k@vS20/05/440500178. ...Mii, you're somewhat involved in this one too, so look forward to it.``
``Huh, Mii-chan's in it too?@k@vS20/02/440200377. Then what about Rena?``
``And what about me?``
``...You're both sitting this one out. Nipah~``
````Whaaaaaaat~?!````
``Wait... so if the main character isn't Kei-chan, then... could it be...?``
``N~fufufufu!@k@vS20/11/440700432. In other words, it's time for the opening act of Kuraudo -- also known as Ooishi -- and his theater of refined elegance~!``
``W-whaaaat?! Wh-where did you come from?!``
``Nfufufufu!@k@vS20/11/440700434. No need to look at me like I'm a ghost.``

``Setting that aside, Chapter Zero -- 'Himatsubushi' -- belongs to us adults.@k@vS20/11/440700436. This marks the start of an adult, moody side of 'Higurashi' that's a bit too mature for the kiddies~! Nahahaha!``
``...Au... Hearing Ooishi-san describe it that way, all I can think of is something indecent.@k@vS20/02/440200380. ...I wonder why?``
``Given his track record... I'm seriously worried he's going to single-handedly raise the age rating of this game.@k@vS20/03/440300481. I dread to think what kind of story this will turn into...``
``...Ah, Ooishi-san?@k@vS20/13/441300066. We are civil servants, you know.@k@vS20/13/441300067. Please try to set a good example for the taxpaying public.``
``...Ah! It's that detective.@k@vS20/02/440200382. Um... may I call you Akasaka-san?``
``Ahahaha, call me whatever you like.@k@vS20/13/441300069. Hello, everyone. Thanks for all your hard work.``
``Thank you too~!@k@vS20/03/440300483. ...Huh? Where's Kei-chan?``
``Maebara-san is apparently already reading ahead in the next script.@k@vS20/13/441300071. Since he doesn't appear in 'Himatsubushi,' he said he wanted to use the time to catch up...``
``I can understand how he feels...@k@vS20/04/440400294. I caught a glimpse of Keiichi-san's script once -- the first half alone was thicker than five phone books.``
``At first I thought, 'Oh, a gift from the staff? How nice!' ...but when I opened the box, it was just this massive brick of paper packed inside.``
``...That's beyond insane.@k@vS20/05/440500181. A script that thick could stop a bullet.``
``No, that's an exagger-- actually, maybe not.@k@vS20/03/440300485. When we tried to punch binding holes in it, the awl snapped clean off at the base.``
changeReason: Old says 'stapler' broke; new correctly identifies the tool as an awl (千枚通し) that snapped at the base.

TextENGNew: ``You've still got it easy, Rika-chan. You have relatively few lines.@k@vS20/02/440200386. Mii-chan and Shii-chan have even more than Rena... hau.``
changeReason: Old says Rika has 'a relatively decent number of lines' which is the opposite of JPN 台詞まだ少ないほう (relatively few lines).

TextENGNew: ``...Speaking of Shii-chan, where is she?``

``Ah, she's still working overtime.@k@vS20/03/440300487. She didn't expect to have a big role so soon, so she was totally unprepared and kept blowing her lines...@k@vS20/03/440300488. Well, she's got no one to blame but herself.``
changeReason: Old omits that Shion kept blowing her lines (ＮＧ連発); new includes this detail from the Japanese.

TextENGNew: ``That said, I have quite a bit of dialogue in 'Himatsubushi' as well.@k@vS20/13/441300073. I'm planning to head back to the studio right after this.``
``Nfufufufu!@k@vS20/11/440700438. In that case, would you mind if we got started soon?@k@vS20/11/440700439. We're a bit pressed for time.``
``Oh, right. Of course.@k@vS20/02/440200389. We'll just have to hold a separate party for Keiichi-kun another day.@k@vS20/02/440200390. Mii-chan, could you pass out the drinks?``
``You got it!@k@vS20/03/440300490. Beers for Ooishi-san and Akasaka-san...@k@vS20/03/440300491. and juice for Rika-chan and Satoko, right?``
``Oh ho ho ho!@k@vS20/04/440400296. As expected, underage drinking is strictly off-limits in a commercial game!``
``...Which means I'm stuck with grape juice instead of wine. Mii.``
``Wait, Rika-chan... don't tell me you've actually had wine--?``
``Mii~, I'm just kidding.@k@vS20/05/440500184. No alcohol until twenty, I promise.``
``Does everyone have a glass?@k@vS20/02/440200393. Then Rena will do the honors.@k@vS20/02/440200394. All right... 'Taraimawashi' is officially over. Great work, everyone~!!``
````Great work~~~!!````
*clap clap clap clap clap*!!
``Anyway, it sounds like Ooishi-san and Akasaka-san have somewhere to be.@k@vS20/03/440300494. So let's do a quick look back at this scenario.@k@vS20/03/440300495. Satoko, anything to say?``
``Hmm~~... there's not much to look back on when I barely had any screen time.``
``...Same here. Mii~``
``As for Rena... well.``

``At first I was just teasing Keiichi-kun when he came out of the library,@k@vS20/02/440200397. but then I was ambushing him outside his house after Ooishi-san's interrogation and practically threatening him... That felt really strange.``
``I wonder if Rena was trying to keep Kei-chan quiet back then?``

``What was Rena's reason for trying to shut Kei-chan down like that?@k@vS20/03/440300498. And what did Rena know about the murders that she was hiding?@k@vS20/03/440300499. ...Without those answers, we can only make wild guesses, not proper deductions.``
``...What a terribly uncooperative scenario. Mii~``

``Oh... there was actually one thing that caught my attention.@k@vS20/13/441300076. When Satoko Houjou-san's aunt was murdered a year ago, both Mion and Shion Sonozaki-san disappeared one after another, right?@k@vS20/13/441300077. What does that mean?``

``Hmm... taking the facts at face value, it suggests they either conspired to murder the aunt, or were involved in some other way...@k@vS20/11/440700442. But with no evidence to confirm it, that's pure speculation.``

``Wasn't there anyone in the village who witnessed something?@k@vS20/04/440400300. Neither Hinamizawa nor Okinomiya is particularly large. Wouldn't it be difficult for something like that to go completely unnoticed?``

``Well, the Sonozaki family and the other great families are practically sacred and untouchable within Hinamizawa.@k@vS20/11/440700444. If the entire village sheltered them, their movements would vanish completely into the dark~.``
``Nfufufufu!``

``And the end result is a catastrophe that wipes out the entire village...@k@vS20/03/440300501. Hmm~~, if you chalk it up to the curse, that's one explanation, but... I have absolutely no idea why events would unfold this way~.``
``The fundamental question is whether these incidents were caused by human hands, or by some genuinely supernatural force.@k@vS20/13/441300079. We need to sort that out first before anything else.``
``And if it turns out it was human-caused, then there must be a 'motive' -- someone who stands to benefit.``
``Someone... who benefits?``

``Exactly.@k@vS20/13/441300082. Every crime has someone who benefits from it.@k@vS20/13/441300083. If we can identify that benefit, the intentions and identities of the people involved will naturally come into focus.``
``Then... when we consider the chain of mysterious deaths, and now the catastrophe that killed every last villager... you're saying someone out there benefits from all of this?``
``...Hmm, honestly, there are far too many unknowns -- both in evidence and in the details of the cases -- to make any proper judgment.``

``In that sense, we first need to examine what happened five years ago, when these incidents began -- the dam opposition movement and its aftermath.@k@vS20/11/440700449. I don't think we'd be too late if we started our reasoning from that point.``
``I agree.@k@vS20/13/441300086. At the current stage, we're just trading baseless theories.@k@vS20/13/441300087. We need to gather more information about these incidents first.``
``...There may be important clues hidden in the other scenarios as well.``
``That's true.@k@vS20/03/440300504. Then let's leave 'Himatsubushi' to Ooishi-san and Akasaka-san, while the rest of us carefully reread the scenarios we appear in and think things through.``
``Well then, please continue to enjoy the rest of the story~.``
